behaviors.description: A file placed inside a subdirectory of the watched root is processed, not only files placed directly in the root directory.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS/REAL-INFRA: this behavior depends on real filesystem watch recursion semantics (OS event delivery from nested paths). A fake watcher can hide real missed-subdirectory events. SYSTEM TRIAD #3 applies because success is an externally visible side effect (output produced) after a real file-system round trip. LLM-DEP and MULTI-STEP do not apply.
behaviors.test_description: Run the daemon against real temp input/output directories. Create a nested subdirectory under the watched root, write a file into that nested path, and assert exactly one transformed output appears in the output directory for that file.

behaviors.description: The deduplication logic correctly discards a second processing request for the same file path within a configurable time window.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: risk is mainly module handoff (event intake -> dedup window store -> pipeline trigger), not OS/network/subprocess. REAL-INFRA does not need real watcher behavior for this specific rule. Rejection gate: System was considered but rejected because behavior text is about path+time-window policy, not real OS event contract; Agentic/Workflow are disqualified since no AI/ML decision exists.
behaviors.test_description: Instantiate real intake + dedup + pipeline modules in-process with a controllable clock. Submit two requests for the same path within the dedup window; assert only one pipeline execution. Advance clock past window and submit again; assert execution occurs.

behaviors.description: When the operating system emits multiple filesystem events for a single file write, the daemon processes the file exactly once.
behaviors.justification: REAL-INFRA: behavior explicitly requires real OS multi-event emission characteristics. Mocking watcher events can miss platform-specific duplication patterns. SYSTEM TRIAD #3 applies (externally visible exactly-once output via round-trip observation). No LLM dependency.
behaviors.test_description: Run daemon with a real filesystem watcher. Perform a write pattern known to trigger multiple events (e.g., write+fsync+rename/append) for one logical file and assert only one transformed output/artifact is produced.

behaviors.description: If the watched directory is removed and then recreated while the daemon is running, the daemon resumes watching the new directory without a restart.
behaviors.justification: REAL-INFRA: directory delete/recreate watcher behavior is OS/inode-level and often fails only with real watchers. In-process fakes can mask re-subscription bugs. SYSTEM TRIAD #3 applies because required success is externally visible processing after real delete/recreate round trip.
behaviors.test_description: Start daemon watching a real directory. Delete the watched directory, recreate it at the same path, then add a new file. Assert daemon processes that new file without process restart.

behaviors.description: When multiple files arrive at the same time, they are processed in the order determined by their creation timestamps.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure is likely queue/order orchestration across intake, metadata lookup, and scheduler modules. REAL-INFRA does not have to be real OS watcher for this specific ordering policy; local in-process wiring can validate ordering logic. Rejection gate: System considered but rejected because behavior text specifies ordering by timestamps, not a required real watcher/OS event contract. No AI/ML dependency, so not Agentic/Workflow.
behaviors.test_description: Wire real intake/order/scheduler/pipeline modules in-process. Submit multiple file-arrival requests at nearly the same time with controlled creation timestamps and verify processing sequence follows timestamp order (including deterministic tie-break rule if equal).

behaviors.description: A file that is still being written to when the arrival event fires is not picked up for processing until writing is complete.
behaviors.justification: REAL-INFRA: this is a real filesystem timing/race condition (partial writes, close/flush visibility). Mocked file I/O often hides this failure mode. SYSTEM TRIAD #3 applies because correctness is proven by observing final processed output corresponds to fully written file after round-trip.
behaviors.test_description: Run daemon on real directories. Begin writing a large file in chunks with delays so arrival fires early; verify daemon does not process during partial state, then completes processing only after final write/close, and output matches full file content/checksum.
